---
title: "nitrix.stats.priors"
generated: true
signature-hash: 0606f7bc556deec9
description: "Lengthscale priors as REML regularisers (the `map_rho=` penalties)."
listing:
  id: nitrix-stats-priors-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-stats-priors/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.stats.priors`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Lengthscale priors as REML regularisers (the `map_rho=` penalties).

A small, curated set of priors on the Gaussian-process lengthscale
$\rho$, each returned as a callable $\rho \mapsto -\log p(\rho)$
suitable for the `map_rho=` argument of [`nitrix.stats.gp_fit`](nitrix-stats-gp/gp_fit.qmd) and
[`nitrix.stats.hgp_fit`](nitrix-stats-hgp/hgp_fit.qmd).  This is the lightweight *penalised / maximum a
posteriori* lengthscale: the REML objective $-2 l_R(\rho)$ gains
$-2 \log p(\rho)$, so the selected $\rho$ is the *maximum a
posteriori* value rather than the pure REML maximiser.  (A full posterior over
$\rho$ is a separate concern, addressed by dedicated probabilistic
programming tools such as Stan or `brms`.)

Each builder returns $-\log p(\rho)$ up to an additive constant (the
normaliser is irrelevant to the argmin), so they compose directly:
    from nitrix.stats import gp_fit, invgamma_prior
    res = gp_fit(Y, x, map_rho=invgamma_prior(a=3.0, b=0.5))

The penalties are pure JAX (they ride the jitted $\rho$-search),
elementwise on a scalar $\rho$.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:__module__ -->
### Which prior?

- [`halfnormal_prior`](nitrix-stats-priors/halfnormal_prior.qmd) -- shrinks large $\rho$ (keeps the GP from
  going near-linear); a soft upper scale.
- [`invgamma_prior`](nitrix-stats-priors/invgamma_prior.qmd) -- penalises small $\rho$ (the usual
  penalised-complexity-style regulariser that stops the lengthscale collapsing
  into noise-fitting); the practical default when $\rho$ is weakly
  identified.
- [`lognormal_prior`](nitrix-stats-priors/lognormal_prior.qmd) -- centres $\rho$ on a prior median
  $\exp(\mu)$ with multiplicative spread; symmetric in
  $\log \rho$.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-stats-priors-symbols}
:::
